mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a three-month window signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which lifts cyclical equity earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during the initial six months of a post-recession recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization expansion, which disproportionately benefits cyclical equities and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no correlation with subsequent 5-day equity returns during historical recovery periods, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding a 2.5 percent year-over-year growth threshold force central bank tightening expectations, which compress equity risk premiums and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally consistently despite sustained inflation above the threshold, or the forward return differential between high-inflation and low-inflation regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarter-over-quarter GDP growth and monthly payroll expansion confirm broad-based economic acceleration, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or GDP and payroll diverge without corresponding equity market reactions.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in retail sales figures indicates strengthening consumer spending momentum, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no directional relationship with retail sales momentum, or the signal produces negative equity returns in more than 50% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline consistently when unemployment falls below the threshold, or the forward return distribution shows no significant difference between sub-5% and above-5% unemployment periods.
